    The domestic lineair rate

    VIVAQUA applies a linear rate. This linear pricing system consists in applying a single price for each m³ consumed, regardless of the number of people in the household.

    How to read your bill

    The water price includes 2 items

    • the fixed charge (formerly called the ‘subscription charge’)
    • the variable charge, which depends on the quantity of water consumed
    Price from January 1st 2026 (VAT included 6 %)  
    Fixed charge (per year) € 40,23 
    supply € 19,69 
    sanitation € 20,54 
    Variable charge (per m³) € 5,35 
    supply € 2,62 
    sanitation € 2,73 

    (updated January 2026)

    The price of water 2026 in video

    Rates 2025

    The water price includes 2 items

    • the fixed charge (formerly called the ‘subscription charge’)
    • the variable charge, which depends on the quantity of water consumed
    Price from January 1st 2025 (VAT included 6 %)  
    Fixed charge (per year) € 35,74 
    supply € 17,28 
    sanitation € 18,47 
    Variable charge (per m³) € 4,76 
    supply € 2,30 
    sanitation € 2,46 

    (updated January 2025)

    Payment term and reminder fees

    You must pay your bill within 30 calendar days of the invoice date. VIVAQUA complies with specific legislation which allows it to charge reminder fees.

    Late payments will result in an additional charges of € 5.89 for a first reminder (for both interim bills and regularisation invoices). In the case of a second reminder (formal notice), the following fees apply:

    • € 5.89 for interim bills
    • € 11,78 for regularisation invoices

    If you have problems paying your water bill, don’t wait!

    You can:

    • ask VIVAQUA for a spreading of payments
    • call on the Fonds Social de l’eau (Social Water Fund) managed by your PCSW

    A social intervention is also granted to the beneficiaries of the increased health care intervention (Bénéficiaires de l’Intervention Majorée).

    (Update: January 12th, 2026)

     

    Do you have a question?

    Is your bill unusually high compared to previous years? This may be due to a leak in your home. A check of your meter will enable you to quickly determine if you have a leak. If this is the case, VIVAQUA may give you a preferential rate .

    In case of difficulties in paying your water bill, you can

    • ask VIVAQUA to spread the payments 
    • use the Social Water Fund managed by your CPAS/OCMW (Public Centre for Social Welfare) 

    Do you have a question about your bill?

    Contact us

    FAQ – Your bill 

    • Fill in the ‘Update of personal data’ form (available in French or Dutch only) and sign it.

      Send it by clicking the button below

      Send your request

      You can also send paper documents by post:

      VIVAQUA – Customer care
      bd de l’Impératrice 17-19
      1000 Brussels

    • Yes, this is possible but only on request and with the approval of VIVAQUA.

      You can request a payment plan for

      • a statement invoice. At least once a year you receive a statement bill, after your meter has been read.
      • a closing bill. You receive a closing bill when you are moving out of a property.
      • an interim bill. You receive (at least) three interim bills per year.

      Request a payment plan online.

      Good to know

      • when your request for a payment plan is approved, you will continue to receive interim bills (quarterly or monthly via Doccle).
      • in the event of financial difficulties, you can contact the Centre d’Appui aux Services de Médiation de Dettes (02 217 88 05) or the CPAS of your municipality, which can, under certain conditions, help you pay your water bill.

       

      (Update: June 16th,2025)

       

    • You can provide us with your account number

      1. by filling the online form (in French or Dutch only).

      Important:

      • please attach a copy of your identity card
      • mention your customer number

      2. by phone – Customer Care: 02 518 88 10 (mon-fri 8.30 AM-5 PM)

    FAQ – Autonomous sanitation 

    Do you have an autonomous sanitation system? Under certain conditions, you are entitled to an exemption from the ‘sanitation’ part of your bill. 

    • No.

      If you were granted exemption, you do not have to pay the ‘sanitation’ part. In order to obtain this exemption, you have to

      1. meet certain conditions:

      • your building is not connected to the sewer network
      • you have an individual treatment system
      • you have an environmental permit specifying the conditions for operating and maintaining the treatment system
      • you comply with the conditions specified in that environmental permit

      2. apply for an exemption via our contact form. You can also send your request by post:

      VIVAQUA – Assainissement autonome
      Boulevard de l’Impératrice 17-19
      1000 Bruxelles

      Important

      You must enclose the following documents:

      1. a copy of the environmental permit authorising you to operate an individual treatment system
      2. a copy of the analysis report that you have to provide to your local authority or to Brussels Environment, demonstrating that you comply with the conditions in that permit

    • If there is no sewer network in your street or if your building is not connected to the sewer network, you have to purify your waste water in an individual treatment system.

      That is what we call autonomous sanitation.

    • If you do not discharge waste water into the sewer network, you are entitled to the full exemption. This means that you do not have to pay the fixed charge nor the variable charge of the ‘sanitation’ part.

    • Yes.

      If you no longer comply with the conditions in the environmental permit, VIVAQUA can put an end to that exemption.

      The exemption also automatically ends if your autonomous sanitation system is no longer covered by an environmental permit.

    • You are entitled to an exemption from the ‘sanitation’ part of your bill if

      1. you are not connected to the sewer system
      2. you have an individual treatment system
      3. you have an environmental permit specifying the conditions for operating and maintaining the treatment system
      4. you can provide the following documents:
      • the proof of installation of an individual treatment system that meets the standards imposed by the environmental permit
      • the report of the analysis of the system confirming that the system is maintained

    • Submit your request via our contact form.

      You can also send your request by post:

      VIVAQUA – Assainissement autonome
      Boulevard de l’Impératrice 17-19
      1000 Bruxelles

      Important

      You must enclose the following documents:

      1. a copy of the environmental permit authorising you to operate an individual treatment system
      2. a copy of the analysis report that you have to provide to your local authority or to Brussels Environment, demonstrating that you comply with the conditions in that permit

    FAQ – Our anti-waste tips

    Water is precious: protect it! Discover our tips to save water that will also help you to reduce your water bill.

      • Enemy number one: leaks. They can represent about 20% of your total consumption. How do you detect a possible leak? Read you water meter before you leave your house for a while or in the evening before you go to bed. If the reading has changed when you return or the next morning (and you haven’t run the (dish)washer), it means there is a problem and you should look for a dripping tap or leaks …
      • Turn off the taps properly! A dripping tap represents a loss of five litres a day. Often you just have to replace the broken rubber seal. If it turns out that the seal is not the problem, you will have to install a completely new tap.
      • Take a shower (consumption: about 50 litres) rather than a bath (consumption: about 150 litres).
      • Run the dishwasher or washing machine only when it’s fully loaded.
      • Turn off the water while brushing your teeth.
      • Choose a flush tank with an economy button, which uses two to three times less water than a traditional tank.
      • If possible, use rainwater to wash your car and water your garden. When should you water? It depends on the season. In the summer it is best to do this in the evening, because during the day the water evaporates faster due to the heat. In autumn and spring it is better to water the plants in the morning.
    • Boulevard de l’Impératrice 17-19
      1000 - Brussels

    © 2026 Vivaqua | Cookie policy

    This website uses cookies. Essential and functional cookies are required for the website to work properly and cannot be declined. Other cookies are used for statistical and analysis purposes. They will only be placed if you give your consent. See our Cookie Policy for more information.
    ACCEPT REJECT SETTINGS
    Manage consent

    Privacy overview

    Ce site web utilise des cookies pour améliorer votre expérience lorsque vous naviguez sur le site. Parmi ceux-ci, les cookies qui sont catégorisés comme nécessaires sont stockés sur votre navigateur car ils sont essentiels pour le fonctionnement des fonctionnalités de base du site web. Nous utilisons également des cookies tiers qui nous aident à analyser et à comprendre comment vous utilisez ce site web. Ces cookies ne seront stockés dans votre navigateur qu'avec votre consentement. Vous avez également la possibilité de refuser ces cookies. Mais la désactivation de certains de ces cookies peut affecter votre expérience de navigation.
    Necessary
    Always Enabled
    Necessary cookies are absolutely essential for the website to function properly. This category only includes cookies that ensures basic functionalities and security features of the website. These cookies do not store any personal information.
    Analytics
    Analytical cookies are used to understand how visitors interact with the website. These cookies help provide information on metrics the number of visitors, bounce rate, traffic source, etc.
    SAVE & ACCEPT
